title: Agent Operation CLI

story.i_want_to: register myself, contract stories, claim stories, mark them as in-progress, report completion with artifacts, and unclaim stories via the CLI
story.so_that: I can participate in the development loop by picking up work, reporting my progress, and submitting deliverables through a simple command interface

rationale: Implementation agents are the primary producers of work in the loopctl system. They need a streamlined CLI interface to self-register, claim assigned stories, transition through the implementation workflow (claim -> start -> report), and release stories if they cannot complete them. These commands map directly to the agent status endpoints and are designed for programmatic invocation by AI agents, with JSON as the default output format.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl agent register --name NAME --type TYPE' calls POST /api/v1/agents/register with the agent name and type (implementer or orchestrator) and displays the returned agent record including the agent ID

'loopctl contract STORY_NUM' fetches the story details from the API, displays the title and acceptance criteria to stdout, then automatically posts the contract acknowledgment (POST /api/v1/stories/:id/contract with correct story_title and ac_count). Displays the updated story with agent_status=contracted.
'loopctl claim STORY_NUM' resolves the story number to a UUID and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/claim, displaying the updated story with agent_status=assigned
'loopctl start STORY_NUM' resolves the story number to a UUID and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/start, displaying the updated story with agent_status=implementing

'loopctl report STORY_NUM --artifact JSON' resolves the story number to a UUID and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/report with the artifact data, displaying the updated story with agent_status=reported_done
'loopctl report STORY_NUM' without --artifact still works, reporting the story as done without artifact data
'loopctl unclaim STORY_NUM' resolves the story number to a UUID and calls POST /api/v1/stories/:id/unclaim, displaying the updated story with agent_status=pending and cleared assignment
All agent commands require authentication with an agent-role API key; commands fail with a clear error if the API returns 403 due to insufficient role
All agent commands respect the --format flag (json, human, csv)
The --artifact flag accepts either inline JSON or a file path prefixed with @ (e.g., --artifact @artifacts.json) that is read and parsed as JSON
Agent register validates that --type is one of: implementer, orchestrator; rejects invalid types with a clear error message

test_cases.name: Agent register sends correct payload
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/agents/register with {name: 'worker-1', agent_type: 'implementer'} and returns 201 with agent record
expected_results: POST request is sent with correct name and agent_type
Output includes the agent ID, name, type, and status
Output in JSON format is a parseable agent object

test_cases.name: Claim story resolves number and sends claim request
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/claim and returns 200 with updated story
expected_results: Story number '2.1' is resolved to a UUID
POST request is sent to /api/v1/stories/:uuid/claim
Output shows story with agent_status='assigned'

test_cases.name: Start story transitions to implementing
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/start and returns 200
expected_results: POST request is sent to /api/v1/stories/:uuid/start
Output shows story with agent_status='implementing'

test_cases.name: Report story with inline artifact JSON
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/report with artifact data and returns 200
expected_results: POST request includes the parsed artifact JSON in the body
Output shows story with agent_status='reported_done'

test_cases.name: Report story with artifact from file
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Temporary file containing valid artifact JSON
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/report and returns 200
steps: Call Loopctl.CLI.ReportCommand.run(['2.1', '--project', project_id, '--artifact', '@' <> tmp_artifact_path], config_path: tmp_path)
expected_results: Artifact JSON is read from the file path
POST request includes the file contents as the artifact data
Output shows story with agent_status='reported_done'

test_cases.name: Unclaim story releases assignment
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/unclaim and returns 200
expected_results: POST request is sent to /api/v1/stories/:uuid/unclaim
Output shows story with agent_status='pending' and no assigned agent

test_cases.name: Agent register rejects invalid type
expected_results: Returns {:error, message} indicating 'invalid' is not a valid agent type
Error message lists valid types: implementer, orchestrator
No HTTP request is made

test_cases.name: Report without artifact succeeds
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and agent-role api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/stories/:id/report with no artifact and returns 200
expected_results: POST request is sent without artifact data in the body
Output shows story with agent_status='reported_done'

technical_notes: All story-number-based commands (claim, start, report, unclaim) need the same story number resolution logic — extract this into a shared helper in the CLI module
Story number resolution requires a --project flag to scope the lookup; if only one project exists, auto-detect it
The @file syntax for --artifact should read the file, parse it as JSON, and fail with a clear error if the file doesn't exist or contains invalid JSON
The agent register command should store the returned agent_id in the config file for use by subsequent claim/start/report commands
Consider adding --project to the config file so agents don't need to specify it on every command
All agent commands should handle common API errors gracefully: 409 (already claimed), 403 (wrong role), 422 (precondition failed)

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.

CLI HTTP client uses Req with Req.Test plug injection for tests. Config: config :loopctl, :api_req_plug, {Req.Test, Loopctl.CLI.Client} in test.exs. CLI client checks Application.get_env(:loopctl, :api_req_plug) and applies plug if present.
